content.body: Pharmaceutical chemistry designs molecules to treat disease. Drugs work by interacting with biological receptors. Drug development involves synthesis, testing, and clinical trials.

Key drug properties (Lipinski Rule of 5):
- Molecular mass < 500 Da
- log P (lipophilicity) ≤ 5
- H-bond donors ≤ 5
- H-bond acceptors ≤ 10

Drug-receptor interaction: Lock-and-key or induced fit
Antagonists: block receptor
Agonists: activate receptor

LD₅₀: dose lethal to 50% of test subjects (toxicity measure)

content.title: Aspirin (Acetylsalicylic Acid)
content.body:
Aspirin's mechanism:
1. Irreversibly inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes
2. COX enzymes normally make prostaglandins
3. Prostaglandins cause pain, fever, inflammation
4. Blocking them reduces these symptoms
5. Also inhibits platelet aggregation → prevents clotting
Side effect: Reduces protective stomach lining (prostaglandins also protect stomach)

questions.question: An agonist drug:
questions.explanation: Agonists bind to and activate receptors, mimicking the natural ligand

questions.question: Aspirin works by inhibiting which enzyme?
questions.explanation: Aspirin irreversibly inhibits COX-1 and COX-2, blocking prostaglandin synthesis

questions.question: LD₅₀ is used to measure:
questions.explanation: LD₅₀ is the lethal dose for 50% of test subjects — lower LD₅₀ means more toxic

questions.question: Lipinski's Rule of 5 predicts:
questions.explanation: The rule of 5 predicts whether a drug will be absorbed effectively when taken by mouth

questions.question: Clinical trials test drugs in which order?
options: Phase I (healthy), II (small patient), III (large patient)
questions.answer: Phase I (healthy), II (small patient), III (large patient)
questions.explanation: Phase I: safety in healthy volunteers; Phase II: efficacy in small patient groups; Phase III: large-scale trials
